State Space Postulate
The state of a system is described by a unit
vector in a Hilbert space ^3Ly4AsTJ

depending on the degree of freedom of the system
being considered,    may be infinite-dimensional ^T4r1OM4t

for describing realistic models of quantum
computation, we will be interested in degrees of
freedom for which the state is described by a
vector in a finite-dimensional (complex) Hilbert
space ^XLi881sU

two-level systems ^grpBN5fK

the state of each two-level system is described
by a vector in a 2-dimensional Hilbert space ^XqlLEDh3

the condition that the state is described by a
unit vector means that ^2sXKWYdx

normalization constraint ^z1tqevn2

the general state of the system is a superposition
of both possible states ^NqtiAAcr

a quantum mechanical two-level system ^ndgjn60C

a single photon that can be found in one of two

the presence or absence of a photon in a

convenient basis ^3HuIRKzD

Evolution Postulate ^argrS132

Measurement Postulate ^YHClIqgd

for a given orthonormal basis                  of a
state space       for a system A, it is possible
to perform a Von Neumann measurement on
system     with respect to the basis B that,
given a state ^u4w5FQ00

outputs a label   with probability        and
leaves the system in state ^gDK1ZfvC

furthermore, given a state ^8LZuexL8

from a bipartite state space              ( the
are orthonormal; the      have unit norm but are

for the state ^wa8pLTye

special kind of projective measurement ^NRRNbMIi

for any decomposition of the identity operator

this measurement projects the input state
